behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the real CLI+git interaction: rename detection and status encoding come from real git behavior (subprocess + repository state), and misclassification can emerge at parse/handoff boundaries only visible with actual git outputs. Rejected Integration because behavior text is about true rename semantics in a repository, not just local mapping logic.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp real git repo, commit baseline, rename a tracked file via git, run the CLI, and assert report has the file only in `renamed` with no corresponding `added`/`deleted` entries.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary diff handling depends on real git diff/subprocess output and edge-case payloads; mocks can hide decoding/crash issues that occur only with real binary diff streams. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp git repo, add/modify a real binary file (e.g., image bytes), run the CLI against those changes, assert process exits successfully and binary file contributes to counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The unborn-HEAD/no-commit condition is a real git state exposed via subprocess exit/status behavior. In-process fakes may miss real command failures and stderr/exit-code handling. Rejected Integration because the key failure is OS subprocess behavior against an actual repo state.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a fresh git repo with zero commits, run the CLI, assert zero-exit and an empty structured report.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end correctness claim against real repository history. It requires real git history creation and CLI execution; module-only wiring tests can pass while subprocess/history interpretation is wrong.
behaviors.test_description: Programmatically build a temp repo with known sequence of add/modify/rename/delete commits, run the CLI over the target window, and assert category counts equal the ground-truth operations performed.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged semantics are git index/worktree behaviors surfaced through real git commands. Fakes can miss real differences in porcelain output and command flags. Rejected Integration because the failure mode is tied to true git state transitions in a repository.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create equivalent change sets once unstaged and once staged, run the CLI in both states, and assert identical structured output.
